explanation: The statement 'When prop_0, the prop_1' expresses that whenever 'prop_0' holds, 'prop_1' should also hold at the same time. This is modeled in LTL as 'G (prop_0 -> prop_1)', meaning globally, whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true.

The phrase 'When prop_0, prop_1.' typically means that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This is captured by the globally operator: at every moment, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold.
The statement 'When prop_0, prop_1' typically means that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 should also hold at the same time. This is captured in LTL as 'globally, if prop_0 then prop_1'.

This statement can be interpreted as: 'Whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at that time.' This is universally quantified over all time: globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must be true at the same moment.
